sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The accuracy_metric class is a specialization of the metric_template and is designed to calculate and record accuracy metrics for GNN-based analyses. It computes the accuracy of edge predictions and the classification of the number of top quarks.

sect1.title: The accuracy_metric Class
sect1.para: The accuracy_metric class inherits from metric_template and implements its virtual methods to provide metric-specific functionality.

listitem.para: accuracy_metric(): The constructor initializes the metric name to "accuracy".
accuracy_metric* clone(): Creates a new instance of the accuracy_metric.

void define_variables(): Registers the output variables for the metric. This includes event-wise and global accuracies for edge predictions and n-top classification, for training, validation, and evaluation modes.
void event(): Writes the event-wise metrics to the output file.
void batch(): This method is not used in this metric.
void end(): This method is called at the end of the processing. It calculates the final global accuracies and writes them to the output file.
void define_metric(metric_t* mtx): This is the core method of the class. It takes a metric_t object containing the GNN predictions and truth information, and calculates the accuracies.

sect2.title: Accuracy Calculation
sect2.para:
The define_metric method performs the following calculations:
N-Top Accuracy: It compares the predicted number of top quarks (the index with the highest score in ntop_scores) with the true number of top quarks for each event and accumulates the results in a confusion matrix (ntop_accuracy).
Edge Accuracy: For each edge in the graph, it compares the predicted edge class (based on edge_sc) with the true edge class (edge_tr) and calculates the event-wise and global edge accuracies.

sect2.title: Output Variables
sect2.para:
The metric outputs the following variables to a ROOT file:
Event-wise:
edge_accuracy: The accuracy of edge predictions for the event.
ntop_scores: The predicted scores for the number of top quarks.
ntop_truth: The true number of top quarks.
Global:
global_edge_accuracy: The overall accuracy of edge predictions.
ntops_accuracy: A vector containing the accuracy for each n-top class.
